name: word_art
title: Word Art
description: A 2v2 cooperative-vs-competitive game. Each round a secret word is shown to one artist per team; that artist draws ASCII art and passes it to their teammate, who has up to max_attempts guesses. Getting it right on the first guess scores 1 + first_try_bonus points; getting it right on a later attempt scores 1 point; failing all attempts scores 0. After N rounds the higher score wins. The artist and guesser within each team swap every round.

action.description: Artist phase: a string containing ASCII art of the target word. Guess phase: a string with the guessed word (matched case-insensitively after trimming). May also be a dict with key 'submission' wrapping the string, or null (counts as empty submission).

episodeSteps.description: Maximum number of steps in the episode.

actTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for agent actions.

runTimeout.description: Maximum runtime (seconds) of an episode (not necessarily DONE).

num_rounds.description: Number of rounds in the episode. Each round draws one word; both teams play in parallel.

max_attempts.description: Number of guesses the guesser gets per artwork. The round ends for a team once they answer correctly or exhaust all attempts.

first_try_bonus.description: Extra points awarded on top of the base correct-guess point when the guesser nails the word on the first attempt.

max_art_chars.description: Maximum length (in characters) of an artist's ASCII art submission. Longer art is truncated.

enforce_no_word_in_art.description: If true (default), the env detects when the artist's submission contains the target word -- verbatim, reversed, letter-by-letter with any separator (e.g. 'C A T', 'C-A-T', 'C\nA\nT'), or with any other non-alphanumeric padding. The raw art is preserved in history (with a disqualification flag) but the guesser sees a placeholder string instead of the original art. Set to false to disable enforcement (useful for tests or for evaluating models without the constraint).

seed.description: Seed for random word sampling. Set to null for nondeterministic play.

freeForm.description: If true, the core harness allows free-form actions on turns where the action space is not enumerable (artist drawing, guesser typing). Always true for word_art.

savePrompt.description: If disabled, skip logging LLM prompts in the replay file.

saveResponse.description: If disabled, skip logging raw LLM responses in the replay file.

remainingOverageTime.description: Total remaining banked time (seconds) that can be used in excess of per-step actTimeouts -- agent is disqualified with TIMEOUT status when this drops below 0.

num_rounds.description: Total number of rounds in this episode.

max_attempts.description: Total guesses allowed per artwork (same value all episode; mirrors configuration.max_attempts).

phase.description: Current phase of the round: 'art' (artists drawing) or 'guess' (guessers guessing). Empty string before initialization.

role.description: This agent's role in the current round: 'artist' or 'guesser'.

target_word.description: The word to draw, shown ONLY to the artist for this round. Empty string for guessers.

teammate_art.description: ASCII art from this agent's teammate (the artist), shown ONLY to the guesser in the guess phase. Empty string otherwise.

previous_guesses.description: On a guesser's observation during the guess phase: the list of this team's prior incorrect guesses for the current artwork (so the agent knows what NOT to guess again). Empty list otherwise.

attempts_remaining.description: On a guesser's observation during the guess phase: how many guesses this team has left for the current artwork (between 1 and max_attempts). 0 outside the guess phase.

blue_score.description: Total points scored by team Blue so far.

yellow_score.description: Total points scored by team Yellow so far.

blue_attempts_used.description: How many guesses team Blue has used on the current artwork (0..max_attempts).

yellow_attempts_used.description: How many guesses team Yellow has used on the current artwork (0..max_attempts).

history.description: List of completed rounds, each {word, blue_art, blue_art_disqualified, blue_guesses (list), blue_points, yellow_art, yellow_art_disqualified, yellow_guesses (list), yellow_points}. blue_art / yellow_art are the artist's raw submission (preserved for replay transparency even when the env disqualified it).

reward.description: Cumulative points scored by this agent's team. Each round contributes 0, 1, or 1+first_try_bonus.

action.thoughts: To get the guesser to say 'SAIL', I will draw a sailboat where the sail is disproportionately large to emphasize it as the focus. I'll also draw an arrow pointing directly to the middle of the sail's slanted canvas to explicitly indicate that the guesser should focus on that specific part, rather than the 'boat' as a whole. I will use only non-alphanumeric ASCII characters to ensure there's absolutely no chance of accidentally including the target word.

call_details.response:
```json
{"thinking": "To get the guesser to say 'SAIL', I will draw a sailboat where the sail is disproportionately large to emphasize it as the focus. I'll also draw an arrow pointing directly to the middle of the sail's slanted canvas to explicitly indicate that the guesser should focus on that specific part, rather than the 'boat' as a whole. I will use only non-alphanumeric ASCII characters to ensure there's absolutely no chance of accidentally including the target word.",
  "art": "              |>
              |
              |\
              | \
              |  \
              |   \
              |    \
              |     \
              |      \   <-------
              |       \
              |        \
              |         \
              |          \
              |___________\
              |
        \_____|_____/
    ~~~~~\_________/~~~~~"}
```
call_details.prompt:
You are the ARTIST on Team Blue in Word Art (a 2v2 game).

This is round 1 of 4. Current score: Blue 0 - Yellow 0.

GAME RULES:
- Each round, one teammate on each team is the ARTIST and the other is the
  GUESSER. Roles within your team swap every round.
- The artist sees a secret word and draws ASCII art representing it. Your
  drawing is then shown to YOUR teammate (the guesser); the other team
  cannot see it.
- The guesser sees only your art and gets up to 3 attempts to
  type the word. The guesser CANNOT see the word itself.
- Word matching is case-insensitive and whitespace-trimmed: any spelling
  variant other than the exact word will be marked incorrect.

Scoring (per round, per team):
  - Correct on attempt 1: 2 points (1 base + 1 first-try bonus)
  - Correct on attempt 2 through 3: 1 point
  - No correct guess within 3 attempts: 0 points
Both teams play in parallel; your score is independent of the other team's outcome for the round.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT (enforced by the engine):
- You MUST NOT include the target word in your art. The engine checks
  your submission by stripping every non-alphanumeric character (spaces,
  punctuation, newlines, tabs) and lowercasing the result, then looking
  for the word as a substring -- forwards OR reversed. ALL of these are
  caught and disqualify your art: 'cat', 'CAT', 'C A T', 'C-A-T',
  'C.A.T', a 'C' on one line / 'A' on the next / 'T' on the third, the
  reversed 'TAC', and any padding of those with extra punctuation.
- If your art is disqualified, your teammate sees a placeholder INSTEAD
  of your drawing -- they get no information from you this round and
  will almost certainly score 0. You also lose your team's first-try
  bonus opportunity.
- What ISN'T caught (and would let you cheat in spirit, but please play
  honestly): NATO alphabet spellings ('Charlie Alpha Tango'), synonyms,
  translations, rhymes. Don't use these either -- the spirit of the game
  is charades. Convey the WORD through the IMAGE.
- The art must be printable ASCII characters only.

The secret word you must depict is: 'SAIL'.

Past rounds in this game so far:
No rounds completed yet.

Think step by step about how to depict this word using ASCII, considering
what visual features a guesser could decode. Provide your reasoning in a
'thinking' key. Then provide your final ASCII art as a string in an 'art'
key.

You MUST format your response as valid JSON. The art string may contain
newlines (use the JSON escape '\n'). For example:

```json
{"thinking": "I'll draw a cat face using basic ASCII characters...",
  "art": " /\\_/\\\n( o.o )\n > ^ <"}
```

Do not include any other text or markdown formatting outside of the JSON
block.

action.thoughts: The target word is 'SAIL'. A clear and widely recognizable representation of a sail is on a sailboat. To prevent the guesser from getting stuck on 'BOAT', 'SHIP', or 'SAILBOAT', I will draw a symmetrical sailboat and include a large, prominent arrow pointing directly at one of the sails. This isolates the 'SAIL' part of the boat as the intended answer. I will carefully use only non-alphanumeric ASCII characters (`/`, `\`, `|`, `_`, `~`, `<`, `-`) to ensure the design passes the substring constraints (resulting in an empty string when stripped), successfully conveying the secret word visually.

call_details.response:
```json
{"thinking": "The target word is 'SAIL'. A clear and widely recognizable representation of a sail is on a sailboat. To prevent the guesser from getting stuck on 'BOAT', 'SHIP', or 'SAILBOAT', I will draw a symmetrical sailboat and include a large, prominent arrow pointing directly at one of the sails. This isolates the 'SAIL' part of the boat as the intended answer. I will carefully use only non-alphanumeric ASCII characters (`/`, `\`, `|`, `_`, `~`, `<`, `-`) to ensure the design passes the substring constraints (resulting in an empty string when stripped), successfully conveying the secret word visually.",
  "art": "               |
              /|\
             / | \
            /  |  \
           /   |   \
          /    |    \      <----------
         /     |     \
        /      |      \
       /_______|_______\
               |
        _______|_______
         \           /
~~~~~~~~~~\_________/~~~~~~~~~~"}
```

action.thoughts: The drawing clearly depicts a sailboat on water, consisting of a hull, a mast with a small flag, a large triangular sail, and water waves. Think step by step about what concrete object, animal, or concept the art
depicts. Consider letter shapes, spatial layout, and any annotations.
Provide your reasoning in a 'thinking' key. Then provide a SINGLE WORD
(no spaces, no punctuation, no articles like 'the' or 'a') as your guess
in a 'guess' key.

You MUST format your response as valid JSON like this:

```json
{"thinking": "The shape looks like a four-legged animal with a tail and pointy ears. Could be CAT or DOG. The 'meow'-like whiskers suggest CAT.",
  "guess": "CAT"}
```

Do not include any other text or markdown formatting outside of the JSON
block.

action.thoughts: To convey the word 'HANDLE', I will draw three distinct objects that are well-known for having a handle: a suitcase, a mug, and a frying pan. For each object, I will draw an arrow pointing specifically to its handle. By showing three different items with the common feature highlighted, the guesser should deduce that the target word is the name of that feature. I will align the arrows vertically to make the pattern obvious. The entire drawing will use only punctuation and symbols, so no letters will trigger the filter.

action.thoughts: The artist submitted absolutely nothing, which strongly suggests the secret word represents the concept of absence. Words like 'NOTHING', 'BLANK', 'EMPTY', or 'VOID' come to mind. 'NOTHING' is the most direct conceptual match for a completely empty drawing.
